images.caption: Figure 1:Accuracy over 50 training steps.CEPO improves faster than GRPO and RLSD, reaching its largest gap around step 40 before partially converging by the final checkpoint.

Figure 2:CEPO training pipeline and its relationship to GRPO and RLSD.Given a questionxx, the policyπθ\pi_{\theta}producesGGrollouts that are
partitioned into correct (G+G^{+}) and wrong (G−G^{-}) sets by a verifiable reward.
CEPO conditions two frozen teachers on a sampled correct rationaler+∈G+r^{+}\in G^{+}and rejected rationaler−∈G−r^{-}\in G^{-}, and defines a per-tokencontrastive evidence deltaΔtCE\Delta_{t}^{\mathrm{CE}}that amplifies advantage at decisive tokens (large|ΔtCE||\Delta_{t}^{\mathrm{CE}}|)
while leaving filler tokens near unit weight. Then, the token-level modulated advantage is plugged into a standard PPO-clipped surrogate.

Figure 3:Hyperparameter sensitivityaveraged across 5 reasoning benchmarks).(a)Constantλ\lambdaschedule:λ=0.5\lambda=0.5peaks at41.40%41.40\%, outperforming GRPO (41.17%41.17\%); sustained high-λ\lambdatraining
(λ=1.0\lambda=1.0) introduces noise that offsets the credit-assignment benefit.(b)Linear-decay schedule fromλ0=1.0\lambda_{0}=1.0: a2525-step warmup
matches the constant-λ\lambdapeak (41.25%41.25\%).(c)Evidence clip boundεw\varepsilon_{w}: performance peaks in[0.4,0.5][0.4,0.5]at42.7%42.7\%(+1.5+1.5pp over GRPO) and degrades at both extremes,
smallεw\varepsilon_{w}collapses CEPO toward GRPO, largeεw\varepsilon_{w}destabilizes the modulated advantage.

Figure 4:Contrastive delta fractions during CEPO training.We track the fraction of tokens assigned positive versus negative
contrastive evidence. Positive-delta mass increases early, while
negative-delta mass decreases.

Figure 5:Token-level credit assignment on a parallelogram problem. Green/red/white denote high, low, and neutral token weights. Numbered regions illustrate three claims: ① RLSD over-credits fluent setup prose, while CEPO suppresses it; ② CEPO localizes blame to the misapplied angle-equality inference instead of diffusing penalties; ③ CEPO sharpens credit on the decisive algebraic derivation (x+4=3​x−6x{+}4=3x{-}6, isolation steps, final answer). The lower CEPO clip rate (49.5% vs. 71.3%) indicates a wider effective dynamic range, consistent with Proposition1.
